 38â†’     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be invoked by the [KtLintRuleEngine]. A [RuleProvider] is able to create a new instance of a [Rule] so
 39â†’     * that it can keep internal state and be called thread-safe manner
 40â†’     */
 41â†’    public val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 42â†’    /**
 43â†’     * The default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the
 44â†’     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 45â†’     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 46â†’     */
 47â†’    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 48â†’    /**
 49â†’     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 50â†’     * property being set in any other way.
 51â†’     */
 52â†’    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 53â†’    /**
 54â†’     * Temporary flag to indicate that kotlin embeddable compiler is to be executed with (default) or without the extension point
 55â†’     * 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler'. This extension point is not (yet) supported in the preview of Kotlin 1.9. Some
 56â†’     * rules might no longer work and throw exceptions at runtime.
 57â†’     * It is unclear whether the extension point will be supported. Disabling this flag on the Kotlin 1.8 compiler has the same effect. As
 58â†’     * of that it can be used to assess the impact, and to fix rules before release of the Ktlint version which will be based on Kotlin 1.9.
 59â†’     */
 60â†’    public val enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint: Boolean = true,
 61â†’    /**
 62â†’     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 63â†’     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 64â†’     */
 65â†’    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 66â†’    /**
 67â†’     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 68â†’     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 69â†’     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 70â†’     * is included.
 71â†’     */
 72â†’    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 73â†’) {
 74â†’    init {
 75â†’        require(ruleProviders.any()) {
 76â†’            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 77â†’        }
 78â†’    }
 79â†’
 80â†’    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 81â†’
 82â†’    internal val editorConfigLoader =
 83â†’        EditorConfigLoader(
 84â†’            fileSystem,
 85â†’            editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 86â†’            editorConfigDefaults,
 87â†’            editorConfigOverride,
 88â†’        )
 89â†’
 90â†’    /**
 91â†’     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 92â†’     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 93â†’     *
 94â†’     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 95â†’     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 96â†’     */
 97â†’    public fun lint(
 98â†’        code: Code,
 99â†’        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
100â†’    ) {
101â†’        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
102â†’
103â†’        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
104â†’        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
105â†’
106â†’        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
107â†’            .visitor()
108â†’            .invoke { rule ->
109â†’                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
110â†’                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
111â†’                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
112â†’                }
113â†’            }
114â†’
115â†’        errors
116â†’            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
117â†’            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
118â†’
119â†’        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
120â†’    }
121â†’
122â†’    /**
123â†’     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
124â†’     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
125â†’     *
126â†’     * Format is repeated until no more changes are made or [MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE] has been reached (whichever happens first). The
127â†’     * [callback] includes the lint violations that were auto-corrected (from any run) and lint violations that could not be auto-corrected
128â†’     * (from the last run).
129â†’     *
130â†’     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
131â†’     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
132â†’     */
133â†’    public fun format(
134â†’        code: Code,
135â†’        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
136â†’    ): String {
137â†’        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
138â†’
139â†’        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
140â†’
141â†’        var currentCode = code
142â†’        var formatRunCount = 0
143â†’        var mutated: Boolean
144â†’        // Track whether any run has ever made changes
145â†’        var wasEverMutated = false
146â†’
147â†’        // Collect errors that were auto-corrected across all runs
148â†’        val allAutoCorrectedErrors = mutableSetOf<LintError>()
149â†’
150â†’        do {
151â†’            formatRunCount++
152â†’            LOGGER.debug { "Format run $formatRunCount of file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'" }
153â†’            val formatResult = formatOnce(currentCode)
154â†’            mutated = formatResult.mutated
155â†’            if (mutated) {
156â†’                wasEverMutated = true
157â†’            }
158â†’
159â†’            // Collect auto-corrected errors from this run
160â†’            formatResult.errors
161â†’                .filter { (_, corrected) -> corrected }
162â†’                .forEach { (error, _) -> allAutoCorrectedErrors.add(error) }
163â†’
164â†’            if (mutated && formatRunCount < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE) {
165â†’                // Rerun format with the result from previous run as input
166â†’                currentCode =
167â†’                    Code.fromSnippetWithPath(
168â†’                        content = formatResult.formattedCode,
169â†’                        filePath = code.filePath,
170â†’                        fileName = code.fileName,
171â†’                        script = code.script,
172â†’                        isStdIn = code.isStdIn,
173â†’                    )
174â†’            } else {
175â†’                // Build final error set: all auto-corrected errors plus non-corrected errors from the final run
176â†’                val allErrors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
177â†’
178â†’                // Add all auto-corrected errors from all runs
179â†’                allAutoCorrectedErrors.forEach { error ->
180â†’                    allErrors.add(Pair(error, true))
181â†’                }
182â†’
183â†’                // Add non-corrected errors from the final run
184â†’                formatResult.errors
185â†’                    .filter { (_, corrected) -> !corrected }
186â†’                    .forEach { (error, _) -> allErrors.add(Pair(error, false)) }
187â†’
188â†’                // Report all errors
189â†’                allErrors
190â†’                    .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
191â†’                    .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
192â†’
193â†’                // Return the final code:
194â†’                // - If nothing was ever mutated, return original content as-is
195â†’                // - If something was mutated, add BOM if needed (formattedCode doesn't have BOM from AST)
196â†’                return if (!wasEverMutated) {
197â†’                    code.content
198â†’                } else if (hasUTF8BOM) {
199â†’                    UTF8_BOM + formatResult.formattedCode
200â†’                } else {
201â†’                    formatResult.formattedCode
202â†’                }.also {
203â†’                    LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
204â†’                }
205â†’            }
206â†’        } while (mutated && formatRunCount < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE)
207â†’
208â†’        // This point should never be reached due to the return inside the loop above, but is required for compilation
209â†’        return currentCode.content
210â†’    }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun formatOnce(code: Code): FormatResult {
213â†’        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
214â†’
215â†’        var tripped = false
216â†’        var mutated = false
217â†’        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
218â†’        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleProviders)
219â†’        visitorProvider
220â†’            .visitor()
221â†’            .invoke { rule ->
222â†’                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
223â†’                    tripped = true
224â†’                    if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
225â†’                        mutated = true
226â†’                        /**
227â†’                         * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
228â†’                         * suppression hints might have changed.
229â†’                         */
230â†’                        ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
231â†’                    }
232â†’                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
233â†’                    errors.add(
234â†’                        Pair(
235â†’                            LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
236â†’                            // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
237â†’                            // does correct the error.
238â†’                            canBeAutoCorrected,
239â†’                        ),
240â†’                    )
241â†’                    // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be different
242â†’                    // from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it cn be helpful to know the exact order in which
243â†’                    // violations are being solved.
244â†’                    LOGGER.trace {
245â†’                        "Format violation: ${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $errorMessage (${rule.ruleId})" +
246â†’                            canBeAutoCorrected.ifFalse { " [cannot be autocorrected]" }
247â†’                    }
248â†’                }
249â†’            }
250â†’        if (tripped) {
251â†’            visitorProvider
252â†’                .visitor()
253â†’                .invoke { rule ->
254â†’                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(
255â†’                        rule,
256â†’                        false,
257â†’                    ) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
258â†’                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
259â†’                        errors.add(
260â†’                            Pair(
261â†’                                LintError(line, col, rule.ruleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
262â†’                                // It is assumed that a rule only corrects an error after it has emitted an
263â†’                                // error and indicating that it actually can be autocorrected.
264â†’                                false,
265â†’                            ),
266â†’                        )
267â†’                    }
268â†’                }
269â†’        }
270â†’
271â†’        val formattedCode =
272â†’            if (mutated) {
273â†’                ruleExecutionContext
274â†’                    .rootNode
275â†’                    .text
276â†’                    .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
277â†’            } else {
278â†’                code.content
279â†’            }
280â†’
281â†’        return FormatResult(
282â†’            formattedCode = formattedCode,
283â†’            errors = errors,
284â†’            mutated = mutated,
285â†’        )
286â†’    }
287â†’
288â†’    private data class FormatResult(
289â†’        val formattedCode: String,
290â†’        val errors: Set<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>,
291â†’        val mutated: Boolean,
292â†’    )
293â†’
294â†’    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
295â†’        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
296â†’        return when {
297â†’            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
298â†’            else -> "\n"
299â†’        }
300â†’    }
301â†’
302â†’    /**
303â†’     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
304â†’     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
305â†’     *
306â†’     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
307â†’     * ```properties
308â†’     * final-newline=true
309â†’     * indent-size=4
310â†’     * ```
311â†’     */
312â†’    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
313â†’        val codeStyle =
314â†’            editorConfigOverride
315â†’                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
316â†’                ?.parsed
317â†’                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
318â†’                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
319â†’        val rules =
320â†’            ruleProviders
321â†’                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
322â†’                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
323â†’                .toSet()
324â†’        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
325â†’            .generateEditorconfig(
326â†’                rules,
327â†’                codeStyle,
328â†’                filePath,
329â†’            )
330â†’    }
331â†’
332â†’    /**
333â†’     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
334â†’     */
335â†’    public fun trimMemory() {
336â†’        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
337â†’    }
338â†’
339â†’    /**
340â†’     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
341â†’     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
342â†’     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
343â†’     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
344â†’     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
345â†’     */
346â†’    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
347â†’
348â†’    /**
349â†’     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
350â†’     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
351â†’     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
352â†’     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
353â†’     */
354â†’    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
355â†’        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
356â†’            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
357â†’        )
358â†’    }
359â†’
360â†’    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
361â†’
362â†’    public companion object {
363â†’        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
364â†’
365â†’        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
366â†’
367â†’        /**
368â†’         * The maximum number of times that format is to be executed on a file in case the file is changed. After executing format, the
369â†’         * resulting code can still contain lint violations that can be autocorrected. In such a case format is re-executed on the resulting
370â†’         * code. To avoid formatting is repeated indefinitely, a maximum number of runs has been set.
371â†’         */
372â†’        private const val MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3
373â†’    }
374â†’}
375â†’

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api
  2â†’
  3â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine.Companion.STDIN_FILE
  4â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.konan.file.file
  5â†’import java.io.File
  6â†’import java.nio.file.Path
  7â†’import kotlin.io.path.pathString
  8â†’
  9â†’/**
 10â†’ * A representation of a block of code. Use one of the factory methods [fromFile], [fromPath], [fromSnippet] or [fromStdin] to instantiate.
 11â†’ */
 12â†’public class Code private constructor(
 13â†’    public val content: String,
 14â†’    public val fileName: String?,
 15â†’    public val filePath: Path?,
 16â†’    public val script: Boolean,
 17â†’    public val isStdIn: Boolean,
 18â†’) {
 19â†’    public fun fileNameOrStdin(): String =
 20â†’        if (isStdIn) {
 21â†’            STDIN_FILE
 22â†’        } else {
 23â†’            fileName.orEmpty()
 24â†’        }
 25â†’
 26â†’    public companion object {
 27â†’        /**
 28â†’         * Create [Code] from a [file] containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are taken
 29â†’         * into account.
 30â†’         */
 31â†’        public fun fromFile(file: File): Code =
 32â†’            Code(
 33â†’                content = file.readText(),
 34â†’                fileName = file.name,
 35â†’                filePath = file.toPath(),
 36â†’                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
 37â†’                isStdIn = false,
 38â†’            )
 39â†’
 40â†’        /**
 41â†’         * Create [Code] from a [path] to a file containing valid Kotlin code or script. The '.editorconfig' files on the path to [file] are
 42â†’         * taken into account. This method is intended to be used in unit tests. In order to work with the Ktlint test file system it needs
 43â†’         * to make additional call to get the file system which makes it slower compared to [fromFile]. Prefer to use [fromFile].
 44â†’         */
 45â†’        public fun fromPath(path: Path): Code {
 46â†’            // Resolve the file based on the file system of the original path given.
 47â†’            val file =
 48â†’                path
 49â†’                    .fileSystem
 50â†’                    .file(path.pathString)
 51â†’            return Code(
 52â†’                content = file.readStrings().joinToString(separator = "\n"),
 53â†’                fileName = file.name,
 54â†’                filePath = path,
 55â†’                script = file.name.endsWith(".kts", ignoreCase = true),
 56â†’                isStdIn = false,
 57â†’            )
 58â†’        }
 59â†’
 60â†’        /**
 61â†’         * The [content] represent a valid piece of Kotlin code or Kotlin script. The '.editorconfig' files on the filesystem are ignored as
 62â†’         * the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the same time respecting the
 63â†’         * '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
 64â†’         */
 65â†’        public fun fromSnippet(
 66â†’            content: String,
 67â†’            script: Boolean = false,
 68â†’        ): Code =
 69â†’            Code(
 70â†’                content = content,
 71â†’                filePath = null,
 72â†’                fileName = null,
 73â†’                script = script,
 74â†’                isStdIn = true,
 75â†’            )
 76â†’
 77â†’        /**
 78â†’         * Create [Code] by reading the snippet from 'stdin'. No '.editorconfig' are taken into account.  The '.editorconfig' files on the
 79â†’         * filesystem are ignored as the snippet is not associated with a file path. Use [Code.fromFile] for scanning a file while at the
 80â†’         * same time respecting the '.editorconfig' files on the path to the file.
 81â†’         */
 82â†’        public fun fromStdin(): Code = fromSnippet(String(System.`in`.readBytes()))
 83â†’
 84â†’        /**
 85â†’         * Create [Code] with new content but preserving the original file metadata (fileName, filePath, script, isStdIn). This is used
 86â†’         * internally to rerun format on modified code.
 87â†’         */
 88â†’        internal fun fromSnippetWithPath(
 89â†’            content: String,
 90â†’            filePath: Path?,
 91â†’            fileName: String?,
 92â†’            script: Boolean,
 93â†’            isStdIn: Boolean,
 94â†’        ): Code =
 95â†’            Code(
 96â†’                content = content,
 97â†’                filePath = filePath,
 98â†’                fileName = fileName,
 99â†’                script = script,
100â†’                isStdIn = isStdIn,
101â†’            )
102â†’    }
103â†’}
